name: Lyndon B. Johnson
description: president of the United States from 1963 to 1969 (1908–1973)
abstract: Lyndon Baines Johnson (August 27, 1908 – January 22, 1973), also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States from 1963 to 1969. Johnson was vice president under John F.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: I called it that because although Lyndon Johnson had passed more laws to help African Americans than any other president, I was wondering if it was expedient because of political reasons or if he really had that in him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: I did make them all with the exception of Lyndon Johnson, who of course, had no inaugural ball because he was sworn in upon the death of President Jack Kennedy.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: But after he was killed and President Johnson came in, and they had to change the style, because there was a different personality in the White House.

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: By this time, Lyndon Johnson had changed all the laws around... 1964, Lyndon Johnson, instrumental in passing the civil rights bill.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Not the President. It was another little cat who was a gambler out there.

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I was also on the education, when Lyndon Johnson appointed me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
